Operation 'getPaymentStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate, but description lacks specificity about what status information is returned and practical use cases. Enhanced description clarifies possible status values and helps AI agents understand when to call this operation (e.g., after payment initiation or to verify completion).

data.current_description: Check the current status of a payment by reference ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve the current status of a payment using its reference ID. Returns the payment status (e.g., pending, completed, failed, cancelled) and associated transaction details. Use this to verify payment completion or troubleshoot failed transactions.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks specificity about what status information is returned and practical use cases. Enhanced description clarifies possible status values and helps AI agents understand when to call this operation (e.g., after payment initiation or to verify completion).

message: Operation 'cancelPayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is too brief and lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies input requirements (reference ID), expected output (cancellation confirmation), state constraints (payment must be cancellable), and side effects (payment cannot be completed, no charge). This helps AI agents understand preconditions and consequences.

data.description_suggestion: Request cancellation of a pending payment by reference ID. Cancels the payment transaction and returns a cancellation confirmation. The payment must be in a cancellable state (e.g., pending or authorized). Once cancelled, the payment cannot be completed and funds will not be charged to the customer.
data.reasoning: Description is too brief and lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies input requirements (reference ID), expected output (cancellation confirmation), state constraints (payment must be cancellable), and side effects (payment cannot be completed, no charge). This helps AI agents understand preconditions and consequences.

message: Operation 'refundPayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Current description is identical to summary and provides no actionable information. Enhanced description explains the operation's purpose, input requirements (reference ID), expected output (refund confirmation), state constraints (payment must be completed), and important side effects (asynchronous processing). This significantly improves AI agent interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Request a refund for a completed payment using its reference ID. Initiates a refund transaction that returns funds to the customer's PicPay wallet. Returns a refund confirmation with transaction details. The payment must be in a completed/settled state. Refunds are processed asynchronously and may take time to appear in the customer's account.
data.reasoning: Current description is identical to summary and provides no actionable information. Enhanced description explains the operation's purpose, input requirements (reference ID), expected output (refund confirmation), state constraints (payment must be completed), and important side effects (asynchronous processing). This significantly improves AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'handleNotification' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Current description is implementation-focused rather than operation-focused, and lacks details about notification content and handling requirements. Enhanced description clarifies that this is a webhook receiver, explains what triggers notifications, what data is included, and best practices (idempotency). This helps AI agents understand the operation's role in the payment flow and how to handle it.

data.current_description: Endpoint to receive PicPay payment status notifications. Implement this on your server.
data.description_suggestion: Webhook endpoint for receiving asynchronous payment status notifications from PicPay. PicPay sends notifications when payment status changes (e.g., completed, failed, cancelled). Your server must implement this endpoint to receive and process these notifications. Notifications include payment reference ID and updated status. Implement idempotent processing to handle potential duplicate notifications.
data.reasoning: Current description is implementation-focused rather than operation-focused, and lacks details about notification content and handling requirements. Enhanced description clarifies that this is a webhook receiver, explains what triggers notifications, what data is included, and best practices (idempotency). This helps AI agents understand the operation's role in the payment flow and how to handle it.
